data.content:
### Create New Payload Database Migration File (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This command generates a new, empty migration file in the specified migrations directory. You can optionally provide a name, otherwise, a timestamp will be used, and flags can be used to skip prompts or force creation.

```text
npm run payload migrate:create optional-name-here
```

--------------------------------

### Create Payload Database Migration

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/templates/with-cloudflare-d1/README.md

Generates new database migration files for the Payload CMS application. This step is crucial before deployment to ensure that any changes made to your Payload configuration, such as new collections or fields, are reflected in your database schema. It prepares the database for updates.

```bash
pnpm payload migrate:create
```

--------------------------------

### Run Pending Payload Database Migrations (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This command executes all migration files that have not yet been applied to the database. It ensures the database schema is up-to-date with the latest definitions.

```text
npm run payload migrate
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Runtime Migrations with Database Adapter in Payload CMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

Demonstrates how to configure Payload CMS to run database migrations at runtime during server initialization. The migrations are imported from the auto-generated index file and passed to the database adapter's prodMigrations property. This approach is ideal for long-running servers or containers but should be avoided for serverless platforms like Vercel due to potential cold start delays.

```typescript
// Import your migrations from the `index.ts` file
// that Payload generates for you
import { migrations } from './migrations'
import { buildConfig } from 'payload'

export default buildConfig({
  // your config here
  db: postgresAdapter({
    //  your adapter config here
    prodMigrations: migrations,
  }),
})
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Package.json Scripts for Payload Postgres Migrations

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This `package.json` excerpt illustrates how to set up build scripts for a Payload application using Postgres, integrating migration management. It defines scripts for development, production builds, a helper for the Payload CLI, and a crucial `ci` script that executes all pending migrations before starting the build process, ideal for CI/CD pipelines.

```json
  "scripts": {
    // For running in dev mode
    "dev": "next dev --turbo",

    // To build your Next + Payload app for production
    "build": "next build",

    // A "tie-in" to Payload's CLI for convenience
    // this helps you run `pnpm payload migrate:create` and similar
    "payload": "cross-env NODE_OPTIONS=--no-deprecation payload",

    // This command is what you'd set your `build script` to.
    // Notice how it runs `payload migrate` and then `pnpm build`?
    // This will run all migrations for you before building, in your CI,
    // against your production database
    "ci": "payload migrate && pnpm build"
  }
```

--------------------------------

### Run Slate to Lexical Migration Script in Payload CMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

Executes a migration that converts all Slate rich text content to Lexical format across collections and globals. The script processes all locales, handles both published and draft documents, recursively processes nested fields, and provides progress logging throughout the migration process.

```typescript
import { getPayload } from 'payload'
import config from '@payload-config'
import { migrateSlateToLexical } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/migrate'

const payload = await getPayload({ config })

await migrateSlateToLexical({ payload })
```

--------------------------------

### Define Payload Migration up and down Functions (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This code snippet illustrates the basic structure of a Payload migration file. It defines `up` and `down` asynchronous functions, where `up` applies database changes and `down` reverts them, both having access to the Payload instance and request object.

```ts
import { MigrateUpArgs, MigrateDownArgs } from '@payloadcms/your-db-adapter'

export async function up({ payload, req }: MigrateUpArgs): Promise<void> {
  // Perform changes to your database here.
  // You have access to `payload` as an argument, and
  // everything is done in TypeScript.
}

export async function down({ payload, req }: MigrateDownArgs): Promise<void> {
  // Do whatever you need to revert changes if the `up` function fails
}
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate Collection Hooks: BeforeDuplicate (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/migration-guide/overview.mdx

This example illustrates migrating the `collection.admin.hooks` property to the new field-level `beforeDuplicate` hook. The `beforeDuplicate` hook now accepts standard field hook arguments and is applied directly to the relevant fields.

```typescript
// collections/Posts.ts
import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'

export const PostsCollection: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'posts',
  admin: {
    hooks: {
-     beforeDuplicate: ({ data }) => {
-       return {
-         ...data,
-         title: `${data.title}-duplicate`
-       }
-     }
    }
  },
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'title',
      type: 'text',
      hooks: {
+      beforeDuplicate: [
+        ({ data }) => `${data.title}-duplicate`
+      ],
      },
    },
  ],
}
```

--------------------------------

### Disable SlateToLexicalFeature Hooks for Migration Script

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

This snippet shows the configuration change for `SlateToLexicalFeature` to disable its `afterRead` hook. Setting `disableHooks: true` is an essential step before running the permanent database migration script. It prevents redundant on-the-fly conversions, improves migration script performance, and ensures direct, clean writes of Lexical data to the database.

```ts
SlateToLexicalFeature({ disableHooks: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Execute SQL Directly with Transaction in Payload SQLite Migration (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This example demonstrates executing raw SQL queries against an SQLite database using Payload's migration system. It utilizes the `db.run` method with `sql` template literal from `@payloadcms/db-sqlite` to perform operations, noting that transactions are disabled by default in SQLite.

```ts
import { type MigrateUpArgs, sql } from '@payloadcms/db-sqlite'

export async function up({ db, payload, req }: MigrateUpArgs): Promise<void> {
  const { rows: posts } = await db.run(sql`SELECT * from posts`)
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run Payload CMS Database Migrations (Postgres)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/templates/website/README.md

This command applies any pending database migrations to your Payload CMS project's Postgres database. It should be run on the server after building the application and before starting it, ensuring the database schema is up-to-date with your latest configuration.

```bash
pnpm payload migrate
```

--------------------------------

### Execute SQL Directly with Transaction in Payload Postgres Migration (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This snippet shows how to perform raw SQL queries against a Postgres database within a Payload migration. It uses the `db.execute` method with `sql` template literal from `@payloadcms/db-postgres` to ensure the query runs within the ongoing transaction.

```ts
import { type MigrateUpArgs, sql } from '@payloadcms/db-postgres'

export async function up({ db, payload, req }: MigrateUpArgs): Promise<void> {
  const { rows: posts } = await db.execute(sql`SELECT * from posts`)
}
```

--------------------------------

### Access MongoDB Directly with Transaction Session in Payload Migration (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This example demonstrates how to interact directly with MongoDB's native driver within a Payload migration. It passes the `session` object from `MigrateUpArgs` to ensure database operations are part of the active transaction.

```ts
import { type MigrateUpArgs } from '@payloadcms/db-mongodb'

export async function up({
  session,
  payload,
  req,
}: MigrateUpArgs): Promise<void> {
  const posts = await payload.db.collections.posts.collection
    .find({ session })
    .toArray()
}
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Payload Cloud Plugin Migration (PNPM & TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/migration-guide/overview.mdx

Handles the migration of the Payload Cloud plugin by first uninstalling the old package and then installing the new one. Subsequently, it updates the `payload.config.ts` file to reflect the new import and plugin function call for Payload 3.0 compatibility.

```bash
pnpm remove @payloadcms/plugin-cloud
```

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/payload-cloud
```

```typescript
// payload.config.ts
- import { payloadCloud } from '@payloadcms/plugin-cloud'
+ import { payloadCloudPlugin } from '@payloadcms/payload-cloud'

buildConfig({
  // ...
  plugins: [
-   payloadCloud()
+   payloadCloudPlugin()
  ]
})
```

--------------------------------

### Create Collection Document

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/local-api/overview.mdx

Creates a new document in a specified collection. Supports file uploads, localization, access control overrides, and document duplication. Returns the created document with all specified fields.

```APIDOC
## Create Collection Document

### Description
Creates a new document in the specified collection. Supports file uploads, email verification control, and document duplication.

### Method
Local API Function Call

### Function
`payload.create(options)`

### Parameters

#### collection
- **Type**: string
- **Required**: Required
- **Description**: The slug of the collection to create a document in.

#### data
- **Type**: object
- **Required**: Required
- **Description**: The document data to create.

#### locale
- **Type**: string
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: Specify locale for the document.

#### fallbackLocale
- **Type**: boolean | string
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: Specify fallback locale behavior.

#### user
- **Type**: object
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: User document to run the operation with.

#### overrideAccess
- **Type**: boolean
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: Skip access control checks.

#### showHiddenFields
- **Type**: boolean
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: Include hidden fields in the response.

#### disableVerificationEmail
- **Type**: boolean
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: If creating verification-enabled auth doc, you can optionally disable the email that is auto-sent.

#### filePath
- **Type**: string
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: If your collection supports uploads, you can upload a file directly through the Local API by providing its full, absolute file path.

#### file
- **Type**: File
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: Alternatively, you can directly pass a File. If file is provided, filePath will be omitted.

#### duplicateFromID
- **Type**: string
- **Required**: Optional
- **Description**: If you want to create a document that is a duplicate of another document, provide the document ID to duplicate.

### Request Example
```js
const post = await payload.create({
  collection: 'posts',
  data: {
    title: 'sure',
    description: 'maybe',
  },
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: false,
  user: dummyUserDoc,
  overrideAccess: true,
  showHiddenFields: false,
  disableVerificationEmail: true,
  filePath: path.resolve(__dirname, './path-to-image.jpg'),
  file: uploadedFile,
  duplicateFromID: 'document-id-to-duplicate',
})
```

### Response
Returns the created document object with all fields.
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Convert Slate Data to Lexical with Custom Converters

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

Shows how to programmatically convert Slate data to Lexical format outside of the automatic migration script. This approach allows direct conversion with custom converters for specific use cases or manual data transformations.

```typescript
import { convertSlateToLexical } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/migrate'

const lexicalData = convertSlateToLexical({
  slateData: mySlateData,
  converters: [...defaultSlateConverters, MyCustomConverter],
})
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Payload npm Script in package.json (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/database/migrations.mdx

This configuration sets up an npm script to easily run Payload commands, ensuring the correct configuration path is used for migrations and other CLI operations.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "payload": "cross-env PAYLOAD_CONFIG_PATH=src/payload.config.ts payload"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Payload Collection for Slate-to-Lexical Preview

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

This TypeScript example demonstrates how to integrate the `SlateToLexicalFeature` into a `richText` field within a Payload CMS `CollectionConfig`. By adding this feature to the Lexical editor's features array, data from Slate is converted on-the-fly during read operations, allowing for a preview of the migration without modifying the database. This setup is crucial for testing content conversion before permanent migration.

```ts
import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload';

import { SlateToLexicalFeature } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/migrate';
import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical';

const Pages: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'pages',
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'content',
      type: 'richText',
      editor: lexicalEditor({
        features: ({ defaultFeatures }) => [
          ...defaultFeatures,
          SlateToLexicalFeature({}),
        ],
      }),
    },
  ],
};
```
